message.content: {"items":[{"title":"Allgemeine Bevölkerungsumfrage der Sozialwissenschaften ALLBUS 1980","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.03536","justification":"ALLBUS is a general population survey for the Federal Republic of Germany including educational attainment, political attitudes, and views on government and institutions; although fielded in 1980, it contains retrospective questions about education and political behaviour referring to the 1960s, making it relevant for studying that decade in Germany."},{"title":"Deutsche Hochschullehrerschaft 1954 und 1964","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2044","justification":"This GESIS dataset surveys German university teaching staff in 1954 and 1964 and focuses on higher education structures, academic careers, and attitudes; the 1964 wave directly covers the 1960s in West Germany and is relevant for higher and further education and orientations within the academic elite toward politics and institutions."},{"title":"Studenten in der Bundesrepublik Deutschland 1967/68","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2039","justification":"This survey of students in the Federal Republic of Germany in 1967/68 covers higher education, social background, and political attitudes, including views on democracy, political participation and institutions; it directly concerns higher education and political behaviour in Germany in the late 1960s."},{"title":"Studenten und politische Partizipation 1969","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2038","justification":"This dataset examines German university students’ political participation and attitudes in 1969, including protest, engagement in organisations, and evaluations of government and political institutions; it relates to higher education and political behaviour in Germany at the end of the 1960s."},{"title":"Politische Einstellungen der Bevölkerung in der Bundesrepublik Deutschland 1966","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2027","justification":"This survey of the West German population in 1966 investigates political attitudes, party support, and views of government and political institutions; it directly addresses political behaviour and attitudes and government systems in Germany in the 1960s, and includes education variables to relate outcomes to educational background."},{"title":"Politische Einstellungen der Bevölkerung in der Bundesrepublik Deutschland 1969","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2030","justification":"This 1969 West German political attitudes survey includes questions on party identification, participation, and evaluations of government and political institutions; it offers data on political behaviour and attitudes in Germany at the close of the 1960s, with background variables including educational attainment."},{"title":"Bundestagswahl 1961 (Repräsentative Wahlstatistik und Wahlsoziologische Umfrage)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2020","justification":"This dataset contains a representative election study for the 1961 Federal Election in West Germany, covering voting behaviour, political attitudes, and evaluation of parties and government; it is directly about political behaviour and attitudes and the political system in early‑1960s Germany, and includes education-related background variables."},{"title":"Bundestagswahl 1965 (Wahlsoziologische Umfrage)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2023","justification":"This election study for the 1965 Federal Election in West Germany captures voting behaviour, political involvement, and perceptions of government and parties; the dataset is central for analysing political behaviour and attitudes toward the political system in mid‑1960s Germany, with respondent education recorded as a key background variable."},{"title":"Bundestagswahl 1969 (Wahlsoziologische Umfrage)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2026","justification":"The 1969 Federal Election study for West Germany includes detailed measures of political behaviour, attitudes, and trust in government and parties; it is highly relevant for examining political behaviour and attitudes in Germany at the end of the 1960s and allows analyses by level of education."},{"title":"Politische Sozialisation von Gymnasiasten 1965","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2040","justification":"This survey of German secondary school pupils (Gymnasiasten) in 1965 explores political socialisation, attitudes toward democracy and institutions, and educational context; it connects further/secondary education with political attitudes and orientations toward government and the political system in mid‑1960s West Germany."}]}
